reasoning:
The base rate from the FRED tool establishes that the WEI on 2025-08-02 was 2.61. This value is historically somewhat elevated compared to the running average over the past year (typically between 1.8 and 2.4). Because 2.61 is above the current expected trend line (which points down slightly to around 2.25 for mid-August), the model estimates roughly a 31% chance that the immediate next week's observation will exceed 2.61. 

Over time, while the long-term expected value remains lower than 2.61, the forecast variance increases significantly. As uncertainty grows with distance into the future, the probability distribution widens, placing more mass in the upper tail above 2.61. This causes the probability of an increase to drift upward toward 0.5 for more distant resolution dates. Furthermore, historical data reveals some winter spikes in the WEI (such as values between 2.7 and 3.2 in late 2024 and early 2025), which increases the likelihood of a higher value for the final January 2026 resolution date, supporting a probability very near 50%.
